mission.title: kubeflow: ambassador and vizier-core not starting
mission.description: ambassador and vizier-core not starting. This issue affects 4+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `0          36m`

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
I am using current master (commit 58022fb) and installed kubeflow on a previously created AWS EKS cluster (thus not using `apply platform` provided by kubeflow). (3x t3.large nodes, tested in regions

steps.title: Apply the fix for ambassador and vizier-core not starting
steps.description: As discussed in #3259 I changed the namespace label in the AWS setup for the kubeflow namespace, so that istio injection is by-default disabled. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kub ```yaml $ kubectl -nkubeflow get po NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE alb-ingress-controller-596688f5bd-9ls7c 2/2 Running 1 46m ambassador-796bcd74b5-4p949 1/2 CrashLoopBackOff 13 46m ambassador-796bcd74b5-gb4pl 1/2 CrashLoopBackOff 13 46m ambassador-796bcd74b5-rxvvb 1/2 CrashLoopBackOff 9 46m app-controller-c6d849967-rmc6q ```

steps.title: Confirm ambassador and vizier-core not starting is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `0          36m` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: As discussed in #3259 I changed the namespace label in the AWS setup for the kubeflow namespace, so that istio injection is by-default disabled. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/3289). This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
